description_long: This is a large, multi-engine airplane with a straight, high-mounted wing configuration and a distinctive twin-boom tail design. The fuselage is robust and streamlined, with a forward-placed, enclosed cockpit featuring multiple windows for visibility. Four engines are mounted on the leading edge of the wing, each with a prominent propeller, indicating a piston engine configuration. The tail section consists of two vertical stabilizers connected by a horizontal stabilizer, forming an H-tail. The airplane's surface appears metallic and smooth, with visible markings and registration numbers on the wings and fuselage.
description: A large, four-engine, high-wing airplane with a twin-boom H-tail and a forward, enclosed cockpit. The surface is metallic and smooth, with visible propellers and registration markings.
description_view_0: Front-left view showing the forward cockpit, four propeller engines, and the twin-boom H-tail configuration.
description_view_1: Rear-left view highlighting the twin vertical stabilizers, horizontal stabilizer, and the high-mounted straight wing.
description_view_2: Rear-right view emphasizing the tail structure and the alignment of the engines along the wing.
description_view_3: Front-right view displaying the cockpit windows, engine arrangement, and the overall wingspan.
attribute_wingspan_configuration: High-wing, straight configuration with no visible winglets or pronounced dihedral/anhedral angles.
attribute_engine_type: Four piston engines mounted on the leading edge of the wing, each with a visible propeller.
attribute_tail_design: H-tail configuration with twin vertical stabilizers connected by a horizontal stabilizer.
attribute_landing_gear_type: Fixed landing gear with multiple main gear units visible under the fuselage and wings.
attribute_cockpit_placement: Forward-mounted, enclosed cockpit with multiple windows, likely multi-seat.
